# `energy/state_peak_electricity_demand_mw`

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by `python -m yen_gov indicator-pages`. Do not hand-edit. -->

**Title**: State peak electricity demand (MW, latest year snapshot)  
**One-line**: Per-state peak instantaneous electricity demand met during the most recent fiscal year.  
**Source artifact last modified**: 2026-05-16 (auto-generated)  
**Source artifact**: [`datasets/indicators/in/energy/state_peak_electricity_demand_mw.json`](../../../../datasets/indicators/in/energy/state_peak_electricity_demand_mw.json)

## Definition

Per-state peak instantaneous electricity demand met during the most recent fiscal year. Tells the citizen how much power the state's grid had to supply at its busiest moment — a service-side counterpart to nameplate capacity.

## Signature

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Entity kind | `state` |
| Time grain | `fiscal_year` |
| Value kind | `count` |
| Unit | `MW` |
| Direction | `neutral` |
| Comparability | `comparable_with_normalisation` |
| Attribution geography | `where_consumed` |
| Implementing authority | `joint` |
| Scale hint | `linear` |

## Coverage

- **Temporal**: 2025-04 (1 period)
- **Spatial**: India (states + UTs) (33 entities)
- **Rows on disk**: 33

## Methodology vintage

ICED powerStatistics endpoint, latest fiscal-year snapshot.

## Notes

Single-year snapshot. Larger states will read higher even when per-capita demand is similar — pair with state population for a per-capita view.
